mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term. A persistently low unemployment rate signals economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:US unemployment rate is 5.6% as of 2015-05-01
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods where UNRATE has been below 6% for more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at historically low levels, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, encouraging risk-taking and driving equity valuations higher over the very short term due to lower discount rates and increased liquidity.
regime_hint: zero lower bound or near-zero interest rate environment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative on average, or not significantly positive, when FEDFUNDS is consistently below 0.25% for any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and increasing housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and investment, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST show recent positive trends
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings are boosted by increased output, leading to higher equity prices. This effect is particularly pronounced when inflation (CPIAUCSL) is stable and not eroding purchasing power, supporting S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth is strong and CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 2% annualized for a sustained period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in GDP reflects a healthy economy, which provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate profitability and, consequently, for S&P 500 equity valuations over the medium term, as future earnings expectations improve.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value is 18666.621 as of 2015-01-01
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positively correlated with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls indicates robust job creation, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the short term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances following a monthly PAYEMS increase exceeding 200,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) suggests a 'goldilocks' scenario where consumer purchasing power is maintained without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average when inflation is low and retail sales growth is strong, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, investor sentiment sours due to concerns about future demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified during economic contractions or recessions.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.
